ai:f.c: Grants exclusive access to objects via unique ID.
ai:f.p: ERR AccessObject(OBJECTID Object, INT MilliSeconds, OBJECTPTR * Result)

ai:i.c: The unique ID of the target object.
The limit in milliseconds before a timeout occurs. The maximum limit is 60000, and 100 is recommended.
A pointer storage variable that will store the resulting object address.

ai:f.c: This function is responsible for executing action routines.
ai:f.p: ERR Action(AC Action, OBJECTPTR Object, APTR Parameters)

ai:i.c: An action or method ID must be specified.
The target object.
Optional parameter structure associated with Action.

ai:f.c: Returns a pointer to the global action table.
ai:f.p: void ActionList(struct ActionTable ** Actions, INT * Size)

ai:i.c: A pointer to the Core's action table struct ActionTable * is returned. Please note that the first entry in the ActionTable list has all fields driven to NULL, because valid action ID's start from one, not zero. The final action in the list is also terminated with NULL fields in order to indicate an end to the list. Knowing this is helpful when scanning the list or calculating the total number of actions supported by the Core.
Total number of elements in the returned list.

ai:f.c: Adds new tags to FileInfo structures.
ai:f.p: ERR AddInfoTag(struct FileInfo * Info, STRVIEW Name, STRVIEW Value)

ai:i.c: Pointer to a valid FileInfo structure.
The name of the tag, which must be declared in camel-case.
The value to associate with the tag name. If empty, any existing tag with a matching Name will be removed.

ai:f.c: Adds a new message handler for processing incoming messages.
ai:f.p: ERR AddMsgHandler(MSGID MsgType, FUNCTION * Routine, struct MsgHandler ** Handle)

ai:i.c: The message type that the handler will intercept. If zero, all incoming messages are passed to the handler.
Refers to the function that will handle incoming messages.
The resulting handle of the new message handler - retain for FreeResource.

ai:f.c: Adjusts the base-line of all log messages.

ai:i.c: The level of adjustment to make to new log messages. Zero is no change. The maximum level is +/- 9.

ai:f.c: Allocates a managed memory block on the heap.
ai:f.p: ERR AllocResource(INT64 Size, MEM Flags, APTR * Address, struct ResourceManager * Manager)

ai:i.c: The size of the memory block in bytes. Must be greater than zero.
Optional allocation flags controlling behaviour and ownership.
Pointer to store the address of the allocated memory block.
Resource manager used to release the resource.

ai:f.c: Generates unique ID's for general purposes.

ai:i.c: The type of ID that is required.

ai:f.c: Analyses paths to determine their type (file, folder or volume).

ai:i.c: The path to analyse.

The result will be stored in the variable referred to by this parameter. The return types are DIRECTORY, FILE and VOLUME. Set this parameter to NULL if you are only interested in checking if the file exists.

ai:f.c: Submit an action for asynchronous execution against an object.
ai:f.p: ERR AsyncAction(AC Action, OBJECTPTR Object, APTR Args, FUNCTION * Callback)

ai:i.c: An action or method ID must be specified here.
The target object to execute the action against.
If the action or method is documented as taking parameters, provide the correct parameter structure here.
Optional function called on the main thread after the action completes.

ai:f.c: Drain the pending queue for listed objects without executing the remaining actions.

ai:i.c: A list of object IDs to cancel.

ai:f.c: Return the number of queued and in-flight async actions for an object.

ai:i.c: The object to query.

ai:f.c: Block until all queued async actions for the listed objects have completed.

ai:i.c: A list of object IDs to wait on.
Maximum time to wait in milliseconds, or -1 for an indefinite wait.

ai:f.c: Broadcast an event to all event listeners in the system.

ai:i.c: Pointer to an event structure.
The size of the Event structure, in bytes.

ai:f.c: Checks objects to see whether or not they support certain actions.

ai:i.c: The target object.
A registered action or method ID.

ai:f.c: Verifies the existence of a resource.

ai:i.c: The unique identifier of the resource to verify.

ai:f.c: Returns an array of all classes known to the system.

ai:i.c: A pointer to an array of class records is returned here.

ai:f.c: Checks if two file paths refer to the same physical file.

ai:i.c: File location 1.
File location 2.

ai:f.c: Makes copies of folders and files.
ai:f.p: ERR CopyFile(STRVIEW Source, STRVIEW Dest, FUNCTION * Callback)

ai:i.c: The source location.
The destination location.
Optional callback for receiving feedback during the operation.

ai:f.c: Makes new folders.

ai:i.c: The location of the folder.
Security permissions to apply to the created Dir(s). Set to NULL if only the current user should have access.

ai:f.c: Creates symbolic links on supported file systems.

ai:i.c: The symbolic link will be created at the location specified here.
The file that you are linking to is specified here.

ai:f.c: Returns a pointer to the object that has the current context.
Returns the active Task object.
Deletes files and folders.

ai:i.c: String referring to the file or folder to be deleted. Folders must be denoted with a trailing slash.
Optional callback for receiving feedback during the operation.

ai:f.c: Deletes volumes from the system.

ai:i.c: The name of the volume.

ai:f.c: Resolves a field ID to its registered name.

ai:i.c: The unique field hash to resolve.

ai:f.c: Returns the internal MetaClass for a given class ID.

ai:i.c: A class ID such as one retrieved from ResolveClassName.

ai:f.c: Finds field descriptors for any class, by ID.
ai:f.p: const struct Field * FindField(OBJECTPTR Object, UINT FieldID, OBJECTPTR * Target)

ai:i.c: The target object.
The hash of the field name to lookup.
(Optional) The object that represents the field is returned here (in case a field belongs to an integrated child object).

ai:f.c: Searches for objects by name.
ai:f.p: ERR FindObject(STRVIEW Name, CLASSID ClassID, OBJECTID * ObjectID)

ai:i.c: The name of an object to search for.
Optional. Set to a class ID to filter the results down to a specific class type.
An object id variable for storing the result.

ai:f.c: Terminates an object by its unique identifier.

ai:i.c: The unique identifier of the object to terminate.

ai:f.c: Safely deallocates resources allocated by AllocResource() and similar functions.

ai:i.c: The unique identifier of the resource to be freed.

ai:f.c: Generates 32-bit IEEE 802.3 CRC checksum values.
ai:f.p: UINT GenCRC32(UINT CRC, APTR Data, UINT Length)

ai:i.c: If streaming data to this function, this value must reflect the most recently returned CRC integer. Otherwise set to zero.
The data to generate a CRC value for.
The length of the Data buffer.

ai:f.c: Returns a message structure if called from an action that was executed by the message system.

ai:i.c: Action identifier represented by the caller.

ai:f.c: Returns the class ID of an ID-referenced object.

ai:i.c: The object to be examined.

ai:f.c: Translates error codes into human readable strings.

ai:i.c: The error code to lookup.

ai:f.c: Generates unique event ID's suitable for event broadcasting.
ai:f.p: INT64 GetEventID(EVG Group, STRVIEW SubGroup, STRVIEW Event)

ai:i.c: The group to which the event belongs.
The sub-group to which the event belongs (case-sensitive).
The name of the event (case-sensitive).

ai:f.c: Returns a direct pointer for any object ID.

ai:i.c: The ID of the object to lookup.

ai:f.c: Returns the unique ID of an object's owner.

ai:i.c: The ID of an object to query.

ai:f.c: Retrieves miscellaneous resource identifiers.

ai:i.c: The ID of the resource that you want to obtain.

ai:f.c: Returns miscellaneous data values from the Core.
Returns the UID of the current thread.
Analyse a file and identify a class that can process it.
ai:f.p: ERR IdentifyFile(STRVIEW Path, CLASSID Filter, CLASSID * Class, CLASSID * SubClass)

ai:i.c: The location of the object data.
Restrict the search to classes in this subset, or use CLASSID::NIL to search all classes.
Must refer to a CLASSID variable that will store the resulting class ID.
Optional argument that can refer to a variable that will store the resulting derived class ID (if the result is a base-class, this variable will receive a value of zero).

ai:f.c: Initialises an object so that it is ready for use.

ai:i.c: The object to initialise.

ai:f.c: Returns a list of all children belonging to an object.

ai:i.c: An object to query.
Must refer to an array of ChildEntry structures.

ai:f.c: Loads files into a local cache for fast file processing.
ai:f.p: ERR LoadFile(STRVIEW Path, LDF Flags, struct CacheFile ** Cache)

ai:i.c: The location of the file to be cached.
Optional flags are specified here.
A pointer to a CacheFile structure is returned here if successful.

ai:f.c: Lock an object to prevent contention between threads.

ai:i.c: The address of the object to lock.
The total number of milliseconds to wait before giving up. If -1, the function will wait indefinitely.

ai:f.c: Moves folders and files to new locations.
ai:f.p: ERR MoveFile(STRVIEW Source, STRVIEW Dest, FUNCTION * Callback)

ai:i.c: The source path.
The destination path.
Optional callback for receiving feedback during the operation.

ai:f.c: Creates new objects.
ai:f.p: ERR NewObject(CLASSID ClassID, NF Flags, OBJECTPTR * Object)

ai:i.c: A class ID from system/register.h or generated by ResolveClassName.
Optional flags.
Pointer to an address variable that will store a reference to the new object.

ai:f.c: Send a notification event to action subscribers.
ai:f.p: void NotifySubscribers(OBJECTPTR Object, AC Action, APTR Args, ERR Error)

ai:i.c: Pointer to the object that is to receive the notification message.
The action ID for notification.
Pointer to an action parameter structure that is relevant to the Action ID.
The error code that is associated with the action result.

ai:f.c: Opens a folder for content scanning.
ai:f.p: ERR OpenDir(STRVIEW Path, RDF Flags, struct DirInfo ** Info)

ai:i.c: The folder location to be scanned. Using an empty string will scan for volume names.
Optional flags.
A DirInfo structure will be returned in the pointer referenced here.

ai:f.c: Returns the context of the client.
Returns the current system time, in microseconds.
Processes system messages that are queued in the task's message buffer.

ai:i.c: Optional flags are specified here (clients should set a value of zero).

A TimeOut value, measured in milliseconds. If zero, the function will return as soon as all messages on the queue are processed. If less than zero, the function does not return until a request for termination is received or a user message requires processing.

ai:f.c: Delay the execution of an action by adding the call to the message queue.
ai:f.p: ERR QueueAction(AC Action, OBJECTID Object, APTR Args)

ai:i.c: The ID of an action or method to execute.
The target object.
The relevant argument structure for the Action, or NULL if not required.

ai:f.c: Reads a file into a buffer.
ai:f.p: ERR ReadFileToBuffer(STRVIEW Path, const std::span<int8_t> &Buffer, INT * Result)

ai:i.c: The path of the file.
Buffer that will receive the file content.
The total number of bytes read into the Buffer will be returned here (optional).

ai:f.c: Read a named tag from a FileInfo structure.
ai:f.p: ERR ReadInfoTag(struct FileInfo * Info, STRVIEW Name, STRVIEW * Value)

ai:i.c: Pointer to a valid FileInfo structure.
The name of the tag, which must be declared in camel-case as tags are case-sensitive.
The discovered string value is returned here if found.

ai:f.c: Registers a file descriptor for monitoring when the task is asleep.
ai:f.p: ERR RegisterFD(HOSTHANDLE FD, RFD Flags, void (*Routine)(HOSTHANDLE, APTR) , APTR Data)

ai:i.c: The file descriptor that is to be watched.
Set to at least one of READ, WRITE, EXCEPT, REMOVE.
The routine that will read from the descriptor when data is detected on it. The prototype is void Routine(HOSTHANDLE FD, APTR Data).
User specific data pointer that will be passed to the Routine. Separate data pointers apply to the read and write states of operation.

ai:f.c: Release a locked object.

ai:i.c: Pointer to the object to be released.

ai:f.c: Resolve a valid CLASSID to its name.

ai:i.c: The ID of the class that needs to be resolved.

ai:f.c: Resolves any class name to a CLASSID UID.

ai:i.c: The name of the class that requires resolution.

ai:f.c: Converts a group ID to its corresponding name.

ai:i.c: The group ID.

ai:f.c: Converts volume-based paths into absolute paths applicable to the host platform.
ai:f.p: ERR ResolvePath(STRVIEW Path, RSF Flags, STRING * Result)

ai:i.c: The path to be resolved.
Optional flags.

Refer to a string variable so that the resolved path can be stored. If NULL, ResolvePath() will work as normal and return a valid error code without the result string. The value is unchanged if the error code is not ERR::Okay.

ai:f.c: Converts a user ID to its corresponding name.

ai:i.c: The user ID.

ai:f.c: Scans the content of a folder, by item.

ai:i.c: Pointer to a DirInfo structure for storing scan results.

ai:f.c: Scans a message queue for multiple occurrences of a message type.
ai:f.p: ERR ScanMessages(INT * Handle, MSGID Type, const std::span<int8_t> &Buffer)

ai:i.c: Pointer to a 32-bit value that must initially be set to zero. The ScanMessages() function will automatically update this variable with each call so that it can remember its analysis position.
The message type to filter for, or zero to scan all messages in the queue.
Optional buffer that is large enough to hold a Message header and any message data.

ai:f.c: Add a message to the local message queue.
ai:f.p: ERR SendMessage(MSGID Type, MSF Flags, const std::span<const int8_t> &Data)

ai:i.c: The message Type/ID being sent. Unique type ID's can be obtained from AllocateID.
Optional flags.
Optional data to copy to the message queue. An empty buffer sends a message without payload data.

ai:f.c: Forces the user and group permissions to be applied to new files and folders.
ai:f.p: void SetDefaultPermissions(INT User, INT Group, PERMIT Permissions)

ai:i.c: User ID to apply to new files.
Group ID to apply to new files.
Permission flags to be applied to new files.

ai:f.c: Register a callback for log messages.
ai:f.p: void SetLogCallback(APTR Callback, INT DepthLimit, INT LogLimit)

ai:i.c: Pointer to the log callback function to register.
Maximum branch depth to forward to the callback.
Maximum message log level to forward to the callback.

ai:f.c: Sets the name of an object.

ai:i.c: The target object.
The new name for the object, or an empty string to clear an existing name.

ai:f.c: Changes object ownership dynamically.

ai:i.c: The object to modify.
The new owner for the Object.

ai:f.c: Updates a writable Core resource value.

ai:i.c: The writable resource identifier.
The value to assign to the resource.

ai:f.c: Redefines the location of a system resource path.

ai:i.c: The ID of the resource path to set.
The new location to set for the resource path.

ai:f.c: Create or modify a filesystem volume.
ai:f.p: ERR SetVolume(STRVIEW Name, STRVIEW Path, STRVIEW Icon, STRVIEW Label, STRVIEW Device, VOLUME Flags)

ai:i.c: Required. The name of the volume.
Required. The path to be associated with the volume. If setting multiple paths, separate each path with a semi-colon character. Each path must terminate with a forward slash to denote a folder.
An icon can be associated with the volume so that it has graphical representation when viewed in the UI. The required icon string format is category/name.
An optional label or short comment may be applied to the volume. This may be useful if the volume name has little meaning to the user (e.g. drive1, drive2 ...).
If the volume references the root of a device, specify a device name of portable, fixed, cd, network or usb.
Optional flags.

ai:f.c: Monitor action calls made against an object.
ai:f.p: ERR SubscribeAction(OBJECTPTR Object, AC Action, FUNCTION * Callback)

ai:i.c: The target object.
The ID of the action that will be monitored. Methods are not supported.
A C/C++ function to callback when the action is triggered.

ai:f.c: Subscribe to a system event.
ai:f.p: ERR SubscribeEvent(INT64 Event, FUNCTION * Callback, APTR * Handle)

ai:i.c: An event identifier.
The function that will be subscribed to the event.
Pointer to an address that will receive the event handle.

ai:f.c: Subscribes an object or function to the timer service.
ai:f.p: ERR SubscribeTimer(DOUBLE Interval, FUNCTION * Callback, APTR * Subscription)

ai:i.c: The total number of seconds to wait between timer calls.
A callback function is required that will be called on each time cycle.
Optional. The subscription will be assigned an identifier that is returned in this parameter.

ai:f.c: Assign a resource manager to an address, or update an existing one.
ai:f.p: ERR TrackResource(RESOURCEID ResourceID, APTR Address, RESOURCEID OwnerID, struct ResourceManager * Manager)

ai:i.c: Unique identifier for the resource to register or replace.
Address of the resource, or NULL to preserve an existing address.
Optional owning resource ID, normally an object. Use 0 when the resource is not owned.
Resource manager used to release the resource.

ai:f.c: Unloads files from the file cache.

ai:i.c: A pointer to a CacheFile structure returned from LoadFile.

ai:f.c: Terminates action subscriptions.
ai:f.p: ERR UnsubscribeAction(OBJECTPTR Object, AC Action, FUNCTION * Callback)

ai:i.c: The object that you are unsubscribing from.
The ID of the action that will be unsubscribed, or zero for all actions.
The original callback reference, or NULL to affect all registrations for the Object/Action combo.

ai:f.c: Removes an event subscription.

ai:i.c: An event handle returned from SubscribeEvent

ai:f.c: Updates the data of any message that is queued.
ai:f.p: ERR UpdateMessage(INT Message, MSGID Type, const std::span<const int8_t> &Data)

ai:i.c: The ID of the message that will be updated.
The type of the message.
Optional replacement data for the message. An empty buffer leaves the existing payload unchanged.

ai:f.c: Modify or remove a subscription created by SubscribeTimer().

ai:i.c: The timer subscription to modify.
The new interval for the timer (measured in seconds), or zero to remove.

ai:f.c: Process incoming messages while waiting on objects to complete their activities.
ai:f.p: ERR WaitForObjects(PMF Flags, INT TimeOut, struct ObjectSignal * ObjectSignals)

ai:i.c: Optional flags are specified here.
A time-out value measured in milliseconds. If this value is negative then no time-out applies and the function will not return until an incoming message or signal breaks it.
A null-terminated array of objects to monitor for signals.

ai:f.c: Waits for a specified amount of seconds.

ai:i.c: The number of seconds to wait for. Fractional values are supported for sub-second precision.

ai:f.c: Interrupt a sleeping thread.

ai:i.c: The target thread's unique ID, as returned by GetThreadID().
If true, the target thread will be put into a stopping state.

ai:k.c: Audio classes interface with audio hardware and drivers for audio playback and recording purposes
Command classes perform specific procedures, like copying or moving a file, managing volumes or executing a program
Data classes parse, query and manipulate data
FileSystem classes are based on file management and interaction with file based data
Graphics classes provide graphics management and drawing services
GUI classes are used in the development of graphical user interfaces
I/O classes manage hardware and software based input and output
Miscellaneous classes do not fit into any of the other available categories
Classes that represent more than one media type, e.g. video files
Network classes interface with system drivers to simplify network communications
System classes are designed to provide low-level services related to system management
Tools provide interactive services for the user
Redirected to a handle
Console created manually
No console available
Launched from a terminal
Android specific events that do not already fit existing categories
Custom event dispatched from an application
Audio system events
Custom event dispatched from a class that doesn't fit within the rest of the event framework
Video display events
File system events
Events generated by the Graphical User Interface
Hardware device events that are not covered by other types
Input/Output events
Network events
Power Management - can also include app-specific events relating to resource management
System-wide events
User activity events (such as user login)
A file is to be, or has been copied.
A file is to be, or has been deleted.
A file is to be, or has been moved.
Function IDs are used to track FUNCTION types and are assigned to the function ID field.
Global IDs have no specific association with anything.
Message IDs are allocated for the purpose of sending uniquely identifiable messages between tasks.
Resource identifier for TrackResource()

ai:c.c: JET constants are documented in GetInputEvent()

ai:k.c: The X, Y values are defined as absolute coordinates, relative to the top-left of the display
Left mouse button; XBox A button, PS square button. Value is pressure sensitive, ranging between 0 - 1.0 (0 is released, 1.0 is fully depressed)
Non-specific button assignment
Right mouse button; XBox X button, PS cross button
Middle mouse button; XBox Y button, PS triangle
Alt. mouse button 1; XBox B button, PS circle
Alt. mouse button 2
Non-specific button assignment
Non-specific button assignment
Non-specific button assignment
Non-specific button assignment
This message is sent by the input system when the mouse pointer enters an area for the first time. The message value refers to the object ID of the container being monitored for movement
This message is sent by the input system when the mouse pointer leaves an area. The message value refers to the object ID of the container being monitored for movement
Controller tilted on the X/Y axis. Value indicates angle, -ve = left, +ve = right
Controller is rising or falling. Value expressed as 'speed',
Recently supplied input occurred at the edge of the display
Pen tilt angle. 0 is pointing down directly with nib at bottom, 0.5 is 90 degrees, 1.0 is inversed (eraser at bottom)
Amount of pressure applied, ranges from 0 (none) to 1.0 (normal) and possibly higher if user presses hard enough
Mouse wheel rotation - the value generally reflects the number of 'clicks' rotated on the wheel
Some mouse wheels can be tilted to the left or right. Ranges from -1.0 to +1.0

ai:c.c: JTYPE flags are used to categorise input types

ai:k.c: Analog movement (ranging from -1.0 to 1.0)
Cursor has been anchored with LockCursor()
Input is a physical button or switch
Crossing events manage the entering and leaving of an area
Set by the input system if the Type is a button and the button has been clicked in quick succession so as to be classed as a double-click
D-Pad or digital joystick source (restricted to +/- 1)
Set if sufficient movement occurred between the original click and its point of release (usually requires a 3 or more pixel difference)
This special flag is set by the input system if the pointer is click-dragging an object at the time of the event
Extended or indirect movement information. This covers all types of movement that are unconnected to coordinate positioning - mouse wheel movement and pen tilt are two such examples
X/Y coordinate movement only. Movement such as the wheel mouse spinning is not covered by this type as it does not influence the coordinate system
Input is a repeated entry (i.e. user is holding down a button and a repetition timer is being triggered)
Indicates to the receiver of this message that it is not the primary/original recipient

Limits the routine to checking the file cache for the existence of the file. If found, the relevant cache entry is returned. The open count is not incremented by this action (it is therefore unnecessary to follow-up with a call to UnloadFile()). If no up-to-date cache entry is available, ERR::Search is returned.
The path refers to a folder.
The path refers to a file.
The path refers to a volume name.
Do not clear the memory on allocation (saves time)
If the Type parameter matches a message already inside the queue, the new message will not be added and the function will immediately return with ERR::Okay.
If Type matches a queued message, update that message's data in place.

ai:c.c: Reserved message ID's that are handled internally.
Flags that can be passed to NewObject(). If a flag needs to be stored with the object, it must be specified in the lower word.

ai:k.c: Read-only indicator that asynchronous actions are queued or executing against this object.
Read-only indicator for when the object is being freed.
Set if FreeResource() was called on an object under lock conditions. Immediately collects when unlocked.
Read-only indicator if the object has been initialised.
Classes can allocate local objects to stop them from being associated with the client.
The object switched from the base-class to a derived class during initialisation.
The object has been signalled and is awaiting processing.
The object is subscribed to a timer interval.
An object created with this flag will not be tracked back to the object that created it.
Synonym for EVERYONE_DELETE
Synonym for EVERYONE_EXEC
Synonym for EVERYONE_READ
Synonym for EVERYONE_WRITE
Marks the file for future backup. The flag should be cleared after the backup has succeeded
Owner can delete. If the file system does not support this, deletion is enabled via the WRITE flag
Synonym for EVERYONE_READ | EVERYONE_WRITE | EVERYONE_EXEC | EVERYONE_DELETE
Synonym for DELETE | GROUP_DELETE | OTHERS_DELETE
Synonym for EXEC | GROUP_EXEC | OTHERS_EXEC
Synonym for READ | GROUP_READ | OTHERS_READ
Synonym for EVERYONE_READ | EVERYONE_WRITE
Synonym for WRITE | GROUP_WRITE | OTHERS_WRITE
User/Owner can execute
Synonym for GROUP_READ | GROUP_WRITE | GROUP_EXEC | GROUP_DELETE
Allows executables to run with a set group id
Group members can delete
Group members can execute
Group members can read
Group members can write
Recommends that the file is hidden from view by default
Inherit permissions from parent folder and logical OR them with preset permission flags
Synonym for HIDDEN | PASSWORD | OFFLINE | NETWORK
File is hosted on another machine
File content for this networked file has not been cached on the local PC
Synonym for OTHERS_READ | OTHERS_WRITE | OTHERS_EXEC | OTHERS_DELETE
Others can delete
Others can execute
Others can read
Others can write
File is password protected
User/Owner has read access. This will not allow compiled code to be executed
Synonym for READ | WRITE | EXEC | DELETE
Allows executables to run with a set user id
Synonym for EXEC
Synonym for READ
Synonym for WRITE
User/Owner can write
Return from WaitForObjects() when any monitored object is signalled.
Set if this is the program's main event loop. Should be accompanied with an infinite timeout.
Retrieve the date stamp of each file.
Read all files in the folder.
Read all files in the folder.
Read all folders/volumes in the folder.
Read all folders/volumes in the folder.
Feedback only - file/folder is actually a link to another location.
Get permission/security information.
Return fully qualified folder names (i.e. trailing slash or colon for each name).
Return fully qualified folder names (i.e. trailing slash or colon for each name).
Synonym for SIZE | DATE | PERMISSIONS | FILES | FOLDERS
Read-only (not permissions related, can indicate read-only media).
Retrieve the byte size of each file.
Path is connected via a stream, e.g. network connection.
Receive additional information for each file, such as comments, author and copyright. The results are stored in the Tags field of each file.
Retrieve the date stamp of each file.
Path is to a virtual device.
Feedback only - indicates a volume.
The average top-speed of all CPU cores in Mhz.
The total amount of free memory.
The total amount of free swap memory.
Return the current JNI environment string.
Maintains the state of key qualifiers such as caps-lock and the shift keys.
The current depth of log messages.
The current level of log detail (larger numbers indicate more detail).
Read-only, returns true if called from the main thread.
Read-only, returns the ID of the main thread.
The total amount of memory used by the current process, in bytes.
This is set to true if the process has elevated privileges (such as superuser or administrative rights).

If this value is set to 1, the process will operate in privileged mode (typically this enables full administrator rights). This feature will only work for Unix processes that are granted admin rights when launched. Setting the Value to 0 reverts to the user's permission settings. SetResource() will return an error code indicating the level of success.
Life-cycle stage of the running process
Returns a map of hashed struct names and corresponding struct sizes.
The total amount of installed memory.
The total amount of shared memory in use (system wide).
The total amount of available swap space.
The Microsoft Windows resource icon ID for this process.
Always call this FD's handler prior to the process going to sleep.
Activate the callback if error conditions are pending.
Activate the callback if there is data available to read.
Set if the subscriber needs to manually check for incoming/outgoing data. This is supported as a one-off check, so the flag will be disabled automatically when the subscriber is called.
Stop monitoring this file descriptor.
Identifies the file descriptor as a socket (Linux systems only).
Activate the callback if there is room to write to the FD's buffer.
An alternative path leading to the system modules (normally system:modules/). Introduced for platforms such as Android, where modules are stored in asset folders.
Overrides the root path, which defaults to the location at which Kōtuku is installed.
The path of the system: volume, which otherwise defaults to [root]:system/.
Ignores file extensions for the purpose of file name matching.
For use on host systems that use case-insensitive file systems such as Windows; this option checks that the discovered file is a case-sensitive match to the Path.

If the volume referenced by Path is traced to another volume that is reserved by a virtual file system driver, ERR::VirtualVolume is returned. The volume is still resolved as far as possible and the resulting path will be returned by this function.
Do not perform more than one iteration when resolving the source file path.
Do not test for the existence of the targeted file or folder during the resolution process.
Use the PATH environment variable to resolve the file name in the Path parameter.
Hides the volume so that it will not show up when reading volumes from the root path :.
If the volume already exists, the path will be inserted at the beginning of the path list so that it has priority over the others.
If the volume already exists, all paths that are attached to it will be replaced with the new path setting.
Identifies the volume as being created by the system (this flag is not for client use).

ai:f.c: Pointer to the function entry point
Action identifier
Hash of the action name.
Byte-size of the structure for this action.
Name of the action.
List of fields that are passed to this action.
Object ID
The class ID of the referenced object.
Unique class identifier (hash of Name)
Parent class ID if this is a derived class
Assigned category
Name of the class
Path to the class file
Wildcards for matching by file extension, e.g. jpeg|jpg
File identification instruction, e.g. [0:$89504e470d0a1a0a]
Icon reference in group/name format
File description
Left-most coordinate
Top coordinate
Right-most coordinate
Bottom coordinate
Right shift value for red (15/16 bit formats only)
Right shift value for green
Right shift value for blue
Right shift value for alpha
Unshifted mask value for red (ranges from 0x00 to 0xff)
Unshifted mask value for green
Unshifted mask value for blue
Unshifted mask value for alpha
Left shift/positional value for red
Left shift/positional value for green
Left shift/positional value for blue
Left shift/positional value for alpha
Number of bits per pixel for this format.
Month 1 to 12
Day 1 to 31
Hour 0 to 23
Minute 0 to 59
Second 0 to 59
TimeZone -13 to +13
Pointer to a FileInfo structure
Left-most coordinate
Top coordinate
Right-most coordinate
Bottom coordinate
Red component value
Green component value
Blue component value
Alpha component value
An option to complement the field type. Can be a pointer or an integer value
A virtual function that will retrieve the value for this field
A virtual function that will set the value for this field
An internal function for writing to this field
The English name for the field, e.g. Width
32-bit hash from fieldhash()
Field offset within the object
Field array index
Special flags that describe the field
The name of the field, e.g. Width
Can be a pointer or an integer value
Special flags that describe the field
The name of the constant.
The value of the constant.
Size of the file
Current seek position within the file if moving or copying
Path to the file
Destination file/path if moving or copying
Set to one of the FBK values
Reserved in case of future expansion
The size of the file's content.
64-bit time stamp - usable only for comparison (e.g. sorting).
Next structure in the list, or NULL.
The name of the file.
Additional flags to describe the file.
Standard permission flags.
User ID (Unix systems only).
Group ID (Unix systems only).
The date/time of the file's creation.
The date/time of the last file modification.
Name of the field
Type of the field
Optional operations for an embedded std::span field
Between 0 and 359.999
Between 0 and 1.0
Between 0 and 1.0. Corresponds to Value, Lightness or Brightness
Alpha blending value from 0 to 1.0
Next event in the chain
The value associated with the Type
PreciseTime() of the recorded input
Surface that the input message is being conveyed to
Surface that is directly under the mouse pointer at the time of the event
Absolute horizontal position of mouse cursor (relative to the top left of the display)
Absolute vertical position of mouse cursor (relative to the top left of the display)
Horizontal position relative to the surface that the pointer is over - unless a mouse button is held or pointer is anchored - then the coordinates are relative to the click-held surface
Vertical position relative to the surface that the pointer is over - unless a mouse button is held or pointer is anchored - then the coordinates are relative to the click-held surface
The hardware device that this event originated from
JET constant that describes the event
Broad descriptors for the given Type (see JTYPE flags). Automatically defined when delivered to the pointer object
Mask to use for checking against subscribers
A timestamp acquired from PreciseTime when the message was first passed to SendMessage.
A unique identifier automatically created by SendMessage.
A message type identifier as defined by the client.
The byte-size of the message data, or zero if no data is provided.
Reference to an object to monitor.
Program name
Path to system files
Path to module files
Kotuku root directory
Command-line arguments
Tag-list of additional options. Typecast to va_list
Client flags indicating the values that have been defined in this structure
Maximum debug depth
Debug detail level (0 none - 9 trace)
Total arguments in Args
Tag identifier
Red component value
Green component value
Blue component value
Alpha component value
Red component value
Green component value
Blue component value
Alpha component value
Red component value
Green component value
Blue component value
Alpha component value
Total colours
RGB Palette
Direct pointer to the resource (optional, can rely on ResourceID instead)
Reference to the resource manager for this record
Unique identifier
Owner of the resource, could be another resource or object
Resource is locked; manager will collect immediately once unlocked
A FreeResource() call currently owns the destruction path
String-based field indicating the user's platform. Currently returns Native, Windows, OSX or Linux.
The Core module's compressed IDL string
The OpenInfo structure originally used to initialise the system
The console type for stdout and stderr, if any
The current operating stage. -1 = Initialising, 0 indicates normal operating status; 1 means that the program is shutting down; 2 indicates a program restart; 3 is for mode switches.
1 = Release build, 0 = Debug build
1 = Static build, 0 = Dynamic build
The unit value.
Additional type information
Byte size of this structure
Format of the audio data
The value(s) associated with the Type
PreciseTime of the recorded input
The hardware device that this event originated from (note: This ID can be to a private/inaccessible object, the point is that the ID is unique)
Broad descriptors for the given Type. Automatically defined when delivered to the pointer object
JET constant
ASCII value of the key A/B/C/D...
PreciseTime at which the keypress was recorded
Unicode value for pre-calculated key translations
Identifier for retrieval from the source
Data preferences for the returned item(s)
